name: Pancorbo — circular from Miranda de Ebro
name_original: Pancorbo — circular desde Miranda de Ebro
region_intro: Touring cycling around Miranda De Ebro offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Ebro River and the Montes Obarenes mountain range. The region features a mix of flat plains, river paths, and challenging climbs through natural parks. Its varied terrain includes paved roads, gravel, and ground tracks, catering to different cycling preferences. The area's lithology, composed of clayey soils, limestones, and sandstones, contributes to the varied riding surfaces. Best touring cycling routes around Miranda De Ebro The most popular touring cycling route is Pancorbo — circular from Miranda de Ebro, a 29.7 miles (47.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Haro – Haro loop from Miranda de Ebro, a moderate 28.9 miles (46.5 km) path. This loop connects Miranda de Ebro with the nearby town of Haro, offering varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Labastida – Carlos III Bridge loop from Miranda de Ebro, a 39.3 miles (63.3 km) trail leading through the region and past the iconic Carlos III Bridge, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Miranda De Ebro is defined by the Ebro River, the Montes Obarenes mountain range, and diverse natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely river paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Miranda De Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Miranda De Ebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Miranda de Ebro?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes available around Miranda de Ebro, catering to various skill levels. This includes a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, allowing cyclists to choose based on their experience and preferences.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Miranda de Ebro?
A: The routes around Miranda de Ebro offer a diverse landscape, shaped by the Ebro River and the Montes Obarenes mountain range. You'll find a mix of flat plains, serene river paths, and challenging climbs through natural parks. The terrain includes paved roads, gravel, and ground tracks, reflecting the region's varied lithology of clayey soils, limestones, and sandstones.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Miranda de Ebro offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. These routes often follow the Ebro River, providing a more relaxed experience. For example, the region has 10 routes classified as easy, offering gentle gradients and scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Miranda de Ebro are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030191639/pancorbo-circular-from-miranda-de-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pancorbo — circular from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.7-mile (47.9 km) route. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1290223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro – Haro loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, which connects to the nearby town of Haro.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Miranda de Ebro offers routes with significant elevation gains, particularly those venturing into the Montes Obarenes mountain range. The region has 46 routes classified as difficult. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24640346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastida – Carlos III Bridge loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.3-mile (63.3 km) trail with substantial climbing.

Q: What can I see or explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore various attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24640346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Labastida – Carlos III Bridge loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; passes the iconic Carlos III Bridge. You can also find historical sites and charming settlements. Nearby attractions include the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro&lt;/a&gt; and various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Velasco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Miranda de Ebro?
A: The diverse climate of Miranda de Ebro, situated between Eurosiberian and Mediterranean biogeographical regions, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While summer can be warm, early mornings are often suitable. Winter cycling is also possible, though some higher elevation routes might be affected by weather.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Miranda de Ebro to other towns or regions?
A: Yes, Miranda de Ebro is a strategic hub for cycling, with routes connecting to nearby towns and regions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1290223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro – Haro loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular example connecting to Haro. The city is also a key point along the Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99), offering opportunities for longer, multi-day touring adventures along the Ebro River.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Miranda de Ebro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Miranda de Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from serene river paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites. Over 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer spectacular views, especially those that ascend into the Montes Obarenes. Peaks like Cruz de Motrico, Gobera, and Peña Escalera provide dramatic backdrops and panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40326219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Ebro Train Station – La Rioja Viewpoint loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint along its path.

Q: Is Miranda de Ebro well-connected for cyclists using public transport?
A: Miranda de Ebro is a significant transport hub, including a train station, which can be a convenient starting point for cycling tours. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda de Ebro Train Station – McDonald's loop from Miranda de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that starts directly from the train station, making it accessible for those arriving by public transport.
tips: The bridge was built in the years 1775-1777 after the very strong flood of 1775 when the original Miranda bridge was destroyed. It connects the Old Town or Aquende neighborhood with Allende - the most modern neighborhood. Sculptures of two lions were placed at the entrance to Allende in 1787. One of the lions holds the royal coat of arms while the other holds that of the city.
It has beautiful views of the city, with an information panel of the names of mountains that can be seen.

Santa Gadea del Cid, walled city in the land of the Ebro
It preserves the medieval street layout and a castle that belonged to the Manrique de Lara family.
On January 17 of each year the festival of San Antón is celebrated in the hermitage.

waypoints.name: Santa Gadea del Cid Village Square
Ermita de San Antón (La Nave), Miranda de Ebro
